# Disclosure Triage Runbook

> Internal handling guide for incoming vulnerability reports: confirm intake, assess impact, decide
> on escalation, and keep the response path consistent with the public policy.

## Intake

Every report starts at the published security contact, `mailto:security@margince.example`. That
contact is the single source for the public policy and this runbook.

## Triage flow

Confirm receipt, reduce the report to the affected surface and impact, reproduce when needed, and
assign ownership for remediation or response. Keep the reporter updated when new information changes
the expected timeline.

## Escalation

If the report indicates an actively exploited vulnerability, follow the CRA Article 14 path and make
the ENISA notification within 24-hour window.

## Closure

Close the loop only after the issue is understood, the response path is chosen, and any external
communications have been made through the same governed channel.
